key_points: Representative values (Mean, Median, Mode) describe the central tendency of data
Arithmetic Mean is the average value of the data set
Range gives the spread of the data (difference between max and min)
Bar graphs use bars of uniform width to represent data visually
examples: Range: In the set [5, 12, 18, 3], Range is $18 - 3 = 15$
Bar Graph: Rainfall in cm for different months represented by vertical bars
Scale: 1 unit = 10 units of data on the Y-axis
formulas: Mean $\rightarrow \frac{\text{Sum of all observations}}{\text{Number of observations}}$
exam_tips: The Mean always lies between the smallest and the largest values of the data
When drawing bar graphs, keep the width of bars and the gaps between them equal
Write the chosen 'Scale' clearly at the top right corner of the graph
Label both the X-axis and Y-axis to make the graph self-explanatory
